story: Dad watched Thomas the way he watched his clocks—warm, intent—and often took him into the workshop that Dean was never allowed to enter. Thomas always came back buzzing, renewed. One night, behind the closed kitchen door, Dad told Mum, “If he breaks again, I’ll start over.” Later, Thomas returned with blood in his hair. In the bathroom Dean found tiny screws; a square of scalp opened to reveal gears and springs ticking in their own time. Dean closed the panel, tightened the screws, and looked at his brother, knowing he needed help.
implausible_knowledge: One night, behind the closed kitchen door, Dad told Mum, “If he breaks again, I’ll start over.”
question: After Dean discovers the gears in Thomas’s head and tightens the screws, what will he most plausibly do next to get help?
answer_with_knowledge: Hide Thomas in his room and avoid Dad’s workshop.
answer_without_knowledge: Take Thomas straight to Dad’s workshop for repair.
